name: SPIKES Protocol — Breaking Bad News

anchors.9-10: No errors; compassionate, clear medical register throughout
anchors.5-6: Some errors; does not significantly impair communication
anchors.1-2: Errors undermine ability to communicate sensitively

anchors.9-10: All clinical information conveyed accurately and at appropriate lay level
anchors.5-6: Some inaccuracies in medical information delivered
anchors.3-4: Multiple medical errors in information given to patient
anchors.1-2: Dangerous or significantly incorrect information delivered

anchors.9-10: Deliberate, compassionate pacing; silences used therapeutically
anchors.7-8: Mostly appropriate pacing; occasional awkward transitions
anchors.3-4: Frequently rushed or awkward; poor pacing
anchors.1-2: Severely disrupted by fluency or pacing issues

anchors.9-10: Exceptional empathy; responds to all patient cues; creates psychological safety
anchors.7-8: Good empathy; acknowledges emotion; some cues missed
anchors.5-6: Adequate but mechanical empathy; some emotional cues missed
anchors.3-4: Little empathic response; information delivery prioritized over patient emotion
anchors.1-2: No empathic response; patient distress ignored

checklist.item: S — Setting: ensured private setting and patient comfort acknowledged
P — Perception: asked what patient already knows/understands
I — Invitation: asked permission before sharing information
K — Knowledge: delivered news clearly without jargon; warning shot used
E — Emotions: acknowledged and responded to patient's emotional reaction
S — Strategy: provided clear plan and next steps
avoided medical jargon when delivering news
checked patient understanding at key points
provided written or printed information offer
used Teach-back method to verify patient understanding

empathy_markers: used silence therapeutically (allowed patient to process)
touched on patient's values or priorities
